legal.title: Support and policies
legal.body: Read how Pet Health Care handles privacy, community terms, and support requests.

home.title: My Pets
home.noPetsBody: When you already have a pet at home, add a profile to keep care records, reminders, and wellness history. If you are still looking, you can browse Pet Feed first.

petFeed.title: Pet Feed
petFeed.subtitle: Structured breeder posts so pet parents can compare important information more clearly than scattered social posts.
petFeed.disclaimer: Listings are provided by breeders. Please verify identity, documents, vaccine records, and arrange an in-person or veterinary check before making decisions.
petFeed.emptyBody: Approved breeder posts will appear here. This MVP avoids in-app payments and only helps you discover and contact breeders.
petFeed.emptyFilteredBody: Try another filter to browse more listings.

topBreeders.emptyBody: After approved listings are available, active breeders will be summarized here.

petFeed.contactConfirmBody: Pet Health Care does not handle payments or verify external conversations. Please verify breeder identity, documents, vaccine records, and meet safely before making decisions.
petFeed.reportSuccess: Thanks. This listing has been sent for admin review.
petFeed.reportBody: Tell admin what looks unsafe, misleading, or abusive about this listing.
petFeed.blockBreederSuccess: This breeder is hidden from your Pet Feed.
petFeed.endOfFeed: You have reached the end of Pet Feed.

breederDetail.title: Breeder profile
breederDetail.trustBody: This score is an in-app reference based on profile transparency and listing activity.
breederDetail.emptyListings: No listings match the current filters.
breederDetail.reportBody: Tell admin what looks unsafe, misleading, or abusive about this breeder profile.
breederDetail.blockBody: You will no longer see this breeder's profile or listings in Pet Feed on this account.

account.title: Account
account.subtitle: Manage your role, saved posts, and app session.
account.adminSubtitle: Manage requests, breeders, listings, and community safety.
account.breederSubtitle: Manage your trusted profile, Pet Feed posts, and review status.

deleteAccount.cardBody: Request permanent deletion of your account and app data. This action cannot be restored from the app.
deleteAccount.title: Delete your account?
deleteAccount.message: Pet Health Care will delete your account profile, pets, health history, Pet Feed activity, and saved listings. This cannot be undone.

requests.title: Requests
requests.body: Breeder verification requests and listing review items grouped by review result.

breeders.title: Breeders
breeders.body: Breeder profiles by operating status so admin can monitor trust and activity.

posts.title: My posts
posts.body: Listings created by the admin account plus review status across the feed.

account.communitySafety: Community posts should stay transparent and respectful. Pet Health Care does not handle payments or replace direct breeder/veterinary verification.
account.breederSafety: Posts should include clear photos, a short video, vaccine/deworming details, and transparent adoption conditions. Avoid absolute health claims without veterinary confirmation.
account.senSummary: Your saved Pet Feed posts and pet care records stay here. You can create a pet profile whenever you are ready.

senIntro.title: Mai's note
senIntro.body: Pet parent, if you want to find more pets, Pet Feed is the best place to browse. If you want to help your pets find a new home, register to upgrade your account to Breeder and wait for admin approval.

helper.unverified: You can submit verification details when you want to create posts for pets looking for a new home.
helper.pending_review: Mai will let you know after admin review. You can still update your details if needed.
helper.verified: This account is eligible for Breeder features.
helper.rejected: Add more trustworthy details and submit again for admin review.
helper.suspended: Breeder access is suspended. Please contact admin if you need support.

breederTrust.missingInfo: Location or main species is missing
breederTrust.note: Transparent details help listings get reviewed faster.

breederPosts.title: My listings
breederPosts.empty: No listings yet. Create your first listing after your profile is verified.

account.vetSummary: Veterinarian features are planned for educational content and care-record review, not medical decisions inside the app.
account.breederOnly: This action is available for Breeder accounts.
account.adminOnly: This action is available for Admin accounts.

breederRequestStatus.pending_review: Breeder request is pending admin review.
breederRequestStatus.rejected: Breeder request was rejected. You can update your information and submit again.

sen.title: Pet parent
sen.body: Browse structured posts, save pets, and keep Care Passport records after bringing a pet home.

breeder.title: Breeder
breeder.body: Create a trustworthy profile and structured pet posts for admin review.

admin.title: Admin
admin.body: Manage accounts, breeder verification, listing review, and community reports.

vet.title: Veterinarian
vet.body: Future role for educational content and record review, not medical decisions inside the app.

breederProfile.title: Breeder registration
breederProfile.maiBody: Pet parent, please provide accurate and complete information to build trust when applying for Breeder access. This verification only applies inside Pet Health Care and helps admins review before allowing Pet Feed posts. Review usually takes about 1 day. If details are unclear, admin may request updates or reject the application.
breederProfile.saved: Breeder information submitted for admin review.
breederProfile.commitmentsRequired: Please accept all transparency commitments before submitting your verification request.

careChecklist.vet_records: Vet records or care documents available
careChecklist.environment_media: Can provide photos/videos of the living environment

commitments.accurate_information: I confirm the provided information is accurate.
commitments.app_only_verification: I understand Breeder verification only applies inside Pet Health Care.

createPetFeedPost.title: Create Pet Feed post
createPetFeedPost.reviewNote: Listings are submitted for review. Be transparent and avoid absolute health claims or misleading content.
createPetFeedPost.adminReviewNote: Admin-created listings publish immediately after confirmation. Review photos, video, and listing details before publishing.
createPetFeedPost.mediaHint: Each listing needs at least 1 clear photo and 1 short video so viewers have better context before contacting.
createPetFeedPost.pricePlaceholder: Enter amount in {{unit}}, or a note

errors.photoRequired: Please upload at least 1 clear photo.

adminReview.title: Admin review
adminReview.roleNote: Admin role is required. Use this console to manage accounts, breeder trust, listings, and reports.

onboarding.introBubbleWelcome:
👋 Welcome to Pet Health Care. 
A place to help you keep, track, and care for your pets' health every day.
onboarding.introBubbleMission: At Pet Health Care, every pet is more than a little companion — they are family. We hope to help you keep every change, habit, and care moment close, so your pets can be loved at the right time.
onboarding.profileGuideByMai: Dear pet parent, please help me fill in your pet's details.
onboarding.servicesWelcomeBody: Pet Health Care uses AI technology for early guidance only. 🐾 It does not replace a veterinarian; if your pet shows serious signs, please seek in-person veterinary care.
onboarding.serviceBreedDesc: AI analyzes photos to suggest dog/cat breed and coat patterns as a reference.
onboarding.serviceHealthDesc: AI reviews photos for early health signs and guidance. It does not replace a veterinarian.
onboarding.serviceVaccineDesc: Track vaccination records digitally and get smart schedule reminders.

healthCheck.title: Wellness Screening for {{name}}
healthCheck.infoBanner: Upload clear photos so Pet Health Care can support an early wellness screening: face, ears, eyes, body... Results are for guidance only.
healthCheck.urgentWarningTitle: Do not wait for AI in urgent situations
healthCheck.urgentWarningBody: If your pet has trouble breathing, collapse, seizure, severe bleeding, repeated vomiting, suspected poisoning, cannot urinate, or is rapidly getting worse, contact a veterinarian or emergency clinic now.
healthCheck.photoCountOne: {{count}} photo — tap area to add more
healthCheck.photoCountMany: {{count}} photos — tap area to add more
healthCheck.medicalHistoryPlaceholder: e.g., known drug allergies or previous skin issues.
healthCheck.symptomsPlaceholder: Describe your pet's condition further (e.g., loss of appetite, lethargy, frequent scratching...)
healthCheck.photoRequiredError: * Please upload at least one photo

cat_rabies.label: Rabies
cat_rabies.detail: Typically given from about 12 weeks of age.

cat_felv.label: FeLV (feline leukemia)
cat_felv.detail: Optional; helps protect against FeLV-related disease.

healthCheck.cooldownHint: Please wait {{seconds}}s before starting another check.
healthCheck.analysisBusyHint: A health check is being processed. Please wait for it to complete.

breedRecognition.title: Breed recognition (preliminary)
breedRecognition.subtitle: Cats and dogs — optional extra context for health checks.
breedRecognition.noteBody: Dear pet parent, this is only a preliminary appearance-based suggestion. It may be wrong for mixed or unknown-lineage pets, and you can always edit your pet's profile manually.
breedRecognition.creditLine: {{remaining}} credits left. Breed recognition costs {{cost}} credit.
breedRecognition.purposeBody: This page collects angle-specific photos (similar to pedigree-style intake) so AI can suggest breed / coat pattern preliminarily. It does not replace a veterinary exam or health analysis.
breedRecognition.accuracyBody: Formal breed registration usually depends on pedigree records, registry documents, microchip/tattoo, DNA/tests, or judge review. Here we only do a lightweight visual step: results are appearance-based and may be wrong for mixed, random-bred, or village pets. You can always edit the profile manually.
breedRecognition.requirementsBody: One photo per slot. Use sharp photos, eye-level angles, a solid contrasting background when possible, and avoid heavy color filters.
breedRecognition.referencesBody: Official registries do not confirm breed from app photos alone, but their rules show which evidence matters: pedigree/registry records, judge review, and clear front/side photos in some alternative programs.

wcf.label: WCF breeding rules
wcf.summary: WCF full/supplementary registers focus on pedigree, breed, colour/pattern, and related identifying traits.

tica.label: TICA cat registration
tica.summary: TICA registration relies on pedigree documents or judge certification for unknown/unregistered parentage.

akcPal.label: AKC PAL photo guidelines
akcPal.summary: AKC PAL asks for clear front and side standing photos for unregistered dogs of registrable breeds.

breedRecognition.needRequiredPhotos: Please add all three required photos: front face, full-body side profile, coat/pattern.
breedRecognition.unsupportedSpeciesAlertBody: This feature is available for cat and dog profiles only.
breedRecognition.applySuccess: Breed field in the profile was updated.

face.title: Front face
face.hint: Clear front view of head, ears, eyes, muzzle/nose.

fullBodySide.title: Full-body side profile
fullBodySide.hint: Standing on a flat surface; whole body visible at pet eye level.

coat.title: Coat and pattern
coat.hint: Body/side close enough to show color, markings, and texture.

eyes.title: Eyes
eyes.hint: Close-up: iris color, shape, set, and clarity.

headProfile.title: Head side profile
headProfile.hint: Side view of nose bridge, forehead, muzzle, and stop/profile.

pawPads.title: Paw pads / toes
pawPads.hint: Underside of paws or toes if possible; useful for some cat traits.

tail.title: Tail
tail.hint: Show length, carriage, shape, and tail coat.

frontFullBody.title: Full front body
frontFullBody.hint: Dog standing from the front; include legs, chest, face, and ears.

ears.title: Ears
ears.hint: Close view of ear set, shape, size, and carriage.

breedRecognitionProgress.title: Mai is scanning the photos
breedRecognitionProgress.subtitle: Please wait while Mai reviews {{name}}'s uploaded angles.
breedRecognitionProgress.resultReadyTitle: Mai has a preliminary breed suggestion
breedRecognitionProgress.resultReadyBody: {{name}}'s result is shown below for you to review.
breedRecognitionProgress.stageBody: Mai is comparing head shape, body proportions, coat pattern, and the uploaded angles to prepare a preliminary suggestion.
breedRecognitionProgress.resultStageBody: Mai has finished reviewing the uploaded photos. This remains a reference suggestion, and you can edit the profile anytime.

breedRecognitionResult.title: Breed Recognition Report
breedRecognitionResult.openSourceBody: This will open a website outside Pet Health Care. Review it carefully before sharing any information.

analysisProgress.title: Reviewing wellness signs
analysisProgress.subtitle: Please wait while we process {{name}}'s data.

stages.analyzing: AI is reviewing signs from photos and notes

results.onboardingSubtitle: Here is the early guidance — tap Finish when you are ready.
results.needMoreDataBody: This result has low certainty. Please add clearer photos or more context.
results.emergencyBody: Potential high-risk signs are present. Please take your pet to the nearest veterinary clinic immediately.
results.fixedDisclaimer: This information is for reference only and does not replace an in-person exam or care plan from a licensed veterinarian.
results.safeFallbackGuidance: This result may contain medical guidance that is too specific for the app to show safely. Please contact a licensed veterinarian and share your pet's photos, symptoms, and timeline.
results.safeFallbackNotice: Some AI wording was hidden because it may sound too definitive or include unsafe medical instructions.

profile.careOverviewHint: A compact view of {{name}}'s records and upcoming care.
profile.deleteHint: Remove {{name}} only if you no longer need this profile or its app records.

vetSummary.title: Vet summary
vetSummary.emptyVisits: No vet visit or document records yet.
vetSummary.disclaimer: This summary is organized owner-entered information and AI check history. It does not replace official veterinary records or an in-person care plan.

coreCare.title: Vaccines & Reminders
coreCare.heroBody: Track shots and reminders so you know what needs attention next.
coreCare.noNextReminder: No upcoming reminders yet. Add vaccine, medication, grooming, or vet follow-up reminders.
coreCare.quickAdd: What would you like to add?
coreCare.manualVaccineHint: If your pet has been vaccinated, you can manually log each shot below.
coreCare.desiredVaccineHint: Choose the main vaccine series you want to schedule first; the app will still add rabies and deworming reminders when appropriate.
coreCare.unsupportedVaccineSpecies: Sample vaccine options are currently available for dogs and cats only.
coreCare.vaccineRequired: Please select a vaccine type for each row.
coreCare.administeredDateRequired: Please select the vaccination date for each row.
coreCare.birthDateRequired: Please select your pet's birth date.
coreCare.desiredVaccineRequired: Please select the vaccine type to schedule.
coreCare.pastDateRequired: Vaccination date must be between the past and today.
coreCare.generatedScheduleIntro: Based on the birth date, Pet Health Care can create guideline-based vaccine and deworming reminders for dogs and cats.
coreCare.generatedScheduleDisclaimer: This is a care reminder, not a diagnosis or prescription. Confirm vaccine products, rabies law, medicine choice, and dose with a veterinarian.
coreCare.generatedScheduleRequired: Please select the birth date and desired vaccine type.
coreCare.generatedScheduleNote: Guideline target: {{targetDate}}. Reference: {{source}}. Based on reference guidelines; this does not replace veterinary advice. Please confirm the product, dose, and local legal requirements with a veterinarian.
coreCare.generatedNextVaccineScheduleNote: Last dose: {{administeredAt}}. Guideline target: {{targetDate}}. Reference: {{source}}. This app creates reminders within the next {{months}} months. Based on reference guidelines; this does not replace veterinary advice. Please confirm product, dose, and local legal requirements with a veterinarian.
coreCare.manualNextVaccineReminderNote: Manual next-dose reminder for {{targetDate}}. Based on reference guidelines; this does not replace veterinary advice. Please confirm the product, dose, and local legal requirements with a veterinarian.
coreCare.felvRiskNote: For FeLV: schedule by risk profile, and consider testing / discussing with a veterinarian first.
coreCare.lapsedPrimarySeriesWarning: Warning: the primary series is overdue for too long after the previous dose and may need to restart from dose 1. Please ask a veterinarian before continuing.
coreCare.preVaccineDewormingNote: A common practice is deworming about 5-7 days before vaccination if the pet has not been dewormed recently or has worm exposure risk. If the pet is weak, vomiting, has diarrhea, or may have a heavy parasite burden, ask a veterinarian first.
coreCare.catchUpScheduleLine: Catch-up reminder: due now because the guideline target age has passed.
coreCare.noGeneratedScheduleBody: Automatic schedules are currently available for dogs and cats with a valid birth date.
coreCare.scheduleAlreadyGeneratedBody: All suggested reminders for this birth date already exist.
coreCare.vaccineScheduleGeneratedBody: Created {{count}} next-dose vaccine reminders for the next 12 months.
coreCare.notePlaceholder: Notes, symptoms, clinic details, document link, or care instructions
coreCare.noReminders: No schedule yet. Add the first vaccine or reminder.
coreCare.creditsBody: {{credits}} AI credits available. Use free care tools anytime; spend credits only when AI is really needed.
coreCare.creditsUnavailable: Credit status is unavailable, but free care tools still work.
coreCare.guidelinesBody: These sources inform the vaccine and deworming reminder formulas. Use them as references and confirm the final plan with a veterinarian.

wsava2024.title: WSAVA 2024 vaccination guidelines
wsava2024.body: Global guidance for core vaccines in dogs and cats, including puppy/kitten series timing.

aahaCanine2022.title: AAHA 2022 canine vaccination guidelines
aahaCanine2022.body: Canine vaccine schedules for DHPP/DAPP, rabies, leptospirosis, and catch-up cases.

aahaAafpFeline2020.title: AAHA/AAFP 2020 feline vaccination guidelines
aahaAafpFeline2020.body: Feline vaccine guidance for FVRCP, rabies, FeLV, kitten series, and booster timing.

troccap.title: TroCCAP tropical parasite guidelines
troccap.body: Parasite control guidance for tropical and subtropical regions.

capc.title: CAPC parasite guidelines
capc.body: Companion animal parasite prevention and deworming recommendations.

emergency.title: Emergency signs
emergency.body: Go to a vet urgently for breathing trouble, collapse, seizures, severe bleeding, repeated vomiting, or suspected poisoning.

vaccines.title: Vaccines and parasite prevention
vaccines.body: Keep vaccine, deworming, and flea/tick dates visible so you do not need AI to remember basic preventive care.

nutrition.title: Nutrition basics
nutrition.body: Track appetite, weight, water intake, and stool changes. Sudden changes are useful context for your vet.

grooming.title: Grooming checklist
grooming.body: Schedule brushing, nail trim, ear cleaning, baths, and skin checks based on your pet's coat and age.

coreCareInfo.title: Additional information
coreCareInfo.heroBody: Vaccination and deworming schedules are not identical for every cat. Review the vaccine type, starting age, prior doses, health status, and lifestyle, then confirm the final plan with a veterinarian.

popularVaccines.title: Common cat vaccines in Vietnam
items: Core multivalent 3-way or 4-way vaccines usually cover feline panleukopenia (FPV), feline herpesvirus/rhinotracheitis (FHV-1), and feline calicivirus (FCV). Some 4-way products may also include Chlamydia felis/psittaci.
Common trade names at clinics include Felocell 4 (Zoetis) and Purevax RCP/RCPCh (Boehringer Ingelheim). Store trade names as notes because clinics may use different products.
Rabies vaccines protect against rabies, a zoonotic disease. Common examples include Rabisin and Defensor 3.
FeLV is generally treated as non-core and is considered for cats with higher exposure risk, outdoor access, multi-cat environments, or known FeLV exposure.

kittenSchedule.title: Kitten primary schedule
items: Kittens usually begin vaccination when maternal antibodies decline, around 6 - 8 weeks of age. Vaccinating too early can reduce effectiveness because maternal antibodies may neutralize the vaccine.
Dose 1 around 6 - 8 weeks: 3-way or 4-way multivalent vaccine.
Dose 2 around 10 - 12 weeks: multivalent vaccine, usually 3 - 4 weeks after the first dose. Rabies may start around 12 weeks/3 months depending on product label and veterinary guidance.
Dose 3 around 14 - 16 weeks: multivalent vaccine to complete the primary series and improve the chance of a full immune response.
FeLV should be risk-based. For at-risk cats, many guidelines recommend FeLV testing first and a 2-dose initial series 3 - 4 weeks apart.

adultBoosters.title: Adult boosters
items: The first booster is usually given about 1 year after the final kitten-series dose, often around 14 - 15 months of age if the kitten finished the final dose at 14 - 16 weeks.
Rabies vaccination should follow local regulations and veterinary guidance. In Vietnam, rabies is part of mandatory disease prevention for companion animals.
Multivalent vaccine boosters can be risk-based: indoor-only cats may be assigned longer intervals, while outdoor cats or cats in multi-cat homes often need closer follow-up.
Booster timing depends on vaccine product, exposure risk, and veterinary assessment, so the app should create reference reminders and let the owner adjust them.

deworming.title: Reference deworming schedule
items: Kittens under 6 months are more vulnerable to worms from the mother or environment. A reference schedule often starts at 3 weeks, then repeats at 5, 7, and 9 weeks.
After the early schedule, monthly deworming may continue until 6 months of age when appropriate for the kitten.
Adult cats over 6 months are often dewormed every 2 - 3 months if they go outdoors, hunt, eat raw meat, or live in a higher-risk environment.
Strictly indoor cats eating cooked/commercial food may be placed on a less frequent schedule, such as every 6 months, based on veterinary guidance.

dataModel.title: How the app should model care schedules
items: The app should not rely only on the most recent vaccine name. It should understand vaccine series and protected components such as FVRCP, Rabies, FeLV, and Deworming.
The next schedule should use the full vaccine/deworming history, current age, most recent date, number of primary-series doses, and the cat's lifestyle risk.
Trade name, manufacturer, clinic, and notes should remain supporting details for owner and veterinarian review.

catFirstYearVaccines.question: What does a kitten usually need in the first year?
answerItems: Core multivalent FVRCP / 3-way or 4-way vaccine: usually starts around 6 - 8 weeks of age, then repeats every 3 - 4 weeks until at least 16 weeks.
Rabies vaccine: usually given from around 12 weeks of age or according to product label and veterinary guidance; in Vietnam this is especially important for rabies prevention.
FeLV: consider by risk profile, such as outdoor access, multi-cat homes, or possible contact with unfamiliar cats; testing / veterinary discussion is recommended first.

Deworming / parasite control: kittens need early repeated deworming by age; if vaccination is coming and the kitten has not been dewormed recently, a common practice is deworming 5 - 7 days before vaccination.
The final schedule should be confirmed with a veterinarian based on health status, lifestyle, and the vaccine product used by the clinic.

dewormBeforeVaccination.question: Should pets be dewormed before vaccination?
answerItems: Yes, this is a common practice when the pet has not been dewormed recently or has worm exposure risk.
A common timing is deworming about 5 - 7 days before vaccination so the pet is more stable before the shot.
If the pet is weak, feverish, vomiting, has diarrhea, or may have a heavy parasite burden, ask a veterinarian before deworming or vaccinating.
If the pet was dewormed properly and recently, the veterinarian may not require another deworming before every vaccine dose.

missedVaccineSchedule.question: What should I do if my pet misses a vaccine date?
answerItems: Do not try to catch up with several shots at once on your own. Contact a clinic so the veterinarian can review age, prior doses, and vaccine product.
Many cases can continue with a catch-up schedule instead of restarting from zero, but this depends on the delay and the vaccine.
In the app, reminders past the guideline target are shown as due/catch-up so the owner can discuss them with a veterinarian.

indoorCatVaccines.question: Does an indoor-only cat still need vaccines?
answerItems: Yes, indoor cats should still have core vaccines such as FVRCP because exposure can happen through clinic visits, grooming, boarding, visitors, or unexpected contact.
Rabies vaccination should follow local rules and veterinary guidance.
Risk-based vaccines such as FeLV may not be needed for every indoor cat, but the decision should be made with a veterinarian.

felvWhenNeeded.question: When should a cat receive FeLV vaccine?
answerItems: FeLV is risk-based and is often considered for cats with outdoor access, multi-cat homes, contact with unfamiliar cats, or known FeLV exposure.
Many guidelines recommend FeLV testing before deciding to vaccinate.
When used, the initial schedule often has 2 doses about 3 - 4 weeks apart, then boosters based on risk and veterinary guidance.

rabiesBoosters.question: What schedule should rabies vaccine and boosters follow?
answerItems: Cats can often receive rabies vaccine from around 12 weeks of age, depending on the vaccine label and veterinarian guidance.
Rabies boosters should follow local rules, vaccine product label, and clinic certificate requirements.
In Vietnam, rabies prevention is especially important because rabies can spread to humans, so owners should confirm the booster plan with a veterinarian.

coreCareInfo.referencesBody: These sources are used to cross-check reminder formulas. App guidance is for reference only and does not replace diagnosis or a care plan from a veterinarian.

wsava2024.title: WSAVA 2024 vaccination guidelines
wsava2024.body: Global guidance on core/non-core vaccines, puppy/kitten timing, and booster principles.

aahaAafpFeline2020.title: AAHA/AAFP 2020 feline vaccination guidelines
aahaAafpFeline2020.body: Feline-specific guidance covering core/non-core categories, FVRCP, FeLV, rabies, and lifestyle-based schedules.

vietnamRabies.title: Vietnam Circular 07/2016/TT-BNNPTNT
vietnamRabies.body: Vietnamese regulation on terrestrial animal disease prevention and control, including rabies prevention for companion animals.

troccapFeline.title: TroCCAP feline parasite guidelines
troccapFeline.body: Parasite control guidance for tropical and subtropical regions, relevant to Southeast Asia.

esccapWorms.title: ESCCAP worm control guidelines
esccapWorms.body: Guidance for controlling worms in dogs and cats, useful for risk-based deworming intervals.
